**Libyan militia commander** Osama Elmasry, accused of overseeing murder, rape, and torture of migrants in detention centres, appeared at the ICC for the first time on Tuesday.

**Arsenal** has won the Premier League for the first time in 22 years, ending a drought that began in 2004.
The **US government** is expanding its white South African refugee programme from 7,500 to 17,500 admissions this year, spending an estimated $100 million.
**Estonia** says a NATO jet shot down a drone over its territory, suspected to be a Ukrainian projectile knocked off course by Russian electronic jamming - the first such incident on Alliance soil.
**Google** will launch its first smart glasses since the 2013 Glass flop this autumn, this time built around Gemini AI for real-time visual assistance.
**UK supermarkets** have flatly rejected the government's push for voluntary price caps on essentials like eggs, bread, and milk, calling the proposal unjustified and likely to push broader costs up.

**Ofcom** ruled TikTok and YouTube are not safe enough for children, an assessment both platforms disputed but which increases regulatory pressure for age-gating legislation.
**Bolivia's** new president Rodrigo Paz reshuffled his cabinet as anti-government protests escalated, marking an early credibility test for the post-Arce government.
**SoftBank** shares surged **20%** after Nvidia's blowout earnings - a proxy for AI momentum given SoftBank's deep stake in Arm Holdings, whose chip designs underpin Nvidia-powered data centres.

**Macron** has said France should address reparations for its role in the slave trade - the first time a sitting French president has used that word without immediately ruling it out.
**Prince Andrew** investigation is widening, with Thames Valley Police appealing for more witnesses in the alleged misconduct in public office inquiry.
**UK melanoma cases** have topped 20,000 for the first time ever, according to Cancer Research UK analysis - a record driven partly by sunbed use and cheap foreign holidays.
**Turkey's** opposition leader Ozgur Ozel vowed to remain in party headquarters after a court ousted him from his post, setting up a direct confrontation with the ruling establishment.

**US arms sale** of $14 billion to Taiwan has been paused, with the acting Navy Secretary citing the ongoing Iran conflict as the reason - a signal Taiwan's security is being traded against Middle East priorities.
